title: Macrame wall hanging with diamonds
subtitle: A 30 cm wide knotted wall piece with diagonal double half-hitch diamond motifs
excerpt: Diamond motifs in macrame are formed with diagonal double half-hitch rows that meet at a central point and then splay outward again, creating a closed diamond shape. This 30 x 50 cm wall hanging combines a row of three diamonds with a fringe finish, worked on 30 cm of mounting cord across a wooden dowel. It is the standard intermediate macrame project for learning diagonal knotting direction and motif repetition.
sourceNotes: Diagonal double half-hitch diamond motifs are the standard intermediate macrame pattern element. Method is observation-led and consistent with contemporary macrame instruction.

content.text: Each diamond is formed by four rows of
: two descending rows that meet at the centre point and two ascending rows that splay outward. The
for each row is pulled at the required angle to control the diagonal direction.

Cut 24 cords, each 5 m long. Fold each in half and mount on the dowel using lark's head knots, placing them evenly across 30 cm of the dowel. You will have 48 working cords. Arrange the dowel at a comfortable working height, hanging from a hook or fixed point.
Step 2: Work the first square-knot row

Work one row of square knots across the full width, grouping cords into sets of 4. This settles the cords and creates a neat first row below the mounting knots. Leave a gap of 3-4 cm below the mounting knots before tying.

Divide the cords into three groups of 16 for three adjacent diamonds. For each diamond: take the leftmost cord as the anchor cord and angle it diagonally down to the right. Knot each of the 7 cords to its right around it using double half hitches (two wraps each). Repeat from the right side: take the rightmost cord as a second anchor and angle it diagonally down to the left. Knot the remaining cords around it. The two anchor cords meet at the centre point. Tie one square knot with the cords at the centre. Then reverse both anchor cords outward to form the lower half of the diamond.

Below the diamonds, work a second row of square knots across the full width, then leave all remaining cords as fringe. Trim the fringe to a consistent length or cut at an angle for a V-shaped finish. Brush the fringe with a stiff bristle brush to separate the fibres and create a fluffy texture, or leave the cords intact for a cleaner look.

items.fix: Hold both anchor cords at symmetrical angles. Pin or clip the anchor cord to a foam board at the correct angle to keep it steady while you work each row.
items.cause: Anchor cord held at different angles on the left and right sides
items.problem: The diamond shape is lopsided, wider on one side than the other

items.fix: After each double half hitch, push the knot firmly up against the previous one before pulling the working cord snug. Consistent upward push produces even spacing.
items.cause: Inconsistent tension: pulling some working cords tighter than others
items.problem: The rows of double half hitches are uneven with loose and tight knots

items.fix: Mark the starting row for each diamond on the cords with a pin or small clip before knotting. All three diamonds should begin from the same horizontal level.
items.cause: Starting the left, centre, and right diamonds at different row heights
items.problem: The three diamonds do not align horizontally
